Meet Juan and Sandra Silva, owners of SJSilva Properties LLC and coaches in the Roadmap to Real Estate Investing. 🏘️

They began as a young couple, tried different businesses until they found real estate. Now they have a thriving portfolio of flips, rentals, and wholesale deals across DFW. Juan is a general contractor handling construction and renovation; Sandra leads operations, acquisitions, and business development. Mentorship changed everything; now they coach other mentees in Deba's program.

feed.title: 🏡 Seller Tip of the Day:…
caps: linkedin

🏡 Seller Tip of the Day:
One of the biggest mistakes I see sellers make is thinking that higher is always better when it comes to pricing. The truth? Buyers are watching the market closely, and an overpriced home can end up costing you time and money.
The goal isn't to list high—it's to list smart. A strategic price can create demand, attract serious buyers, and help you get the best possible outcome.
Would you rather price your home based on what you hope it's worth or what the market is willing to pay? 👇

feed.title: Delays cost time AND money. If…
caps: instagram

Delays cost time AND money. If your draw process is holding up your projects, it is time for a serious upgrade. 

I always recommend Sitewire to my mentees and fellow flippers because it turns a week-long wait into a same-day turnaround.

Watching my mentees use it out in the field, the process is an absolute no-brainer: 
1️⃣ Borrower submits proof of work right from the job site. 
2️⃣ An expert GC verifies the work remotely. 
3️⃣ The lender approves the draw via their dashboard. 
4️⃣ The funds get transferred same-day!
If your lender is still making you wait days for an inspector to show up, you’re losing money. 

Take control of your projects and tell your lender to look into Sitewire! Let’s get these deals closed faster! 🚀

feed.title: Many sellers think they can start…
caps: linkedin

Many sellers think they can start high and lower the price later if needed. But in real estate, first impressions matter! 👀
When a home first hits the market, that's when it attracts the most interest from serious buyers. If it's priced right and presented well from the beginning, you're more likely to generate strong interest and achieve your selling goals.
Preparation isn't just important—it's profitable. 💡🏡

caps: linkedin

💡 Myth or Fact Question of the Day 💡
Many homeowners assume that a cash offer is automatically the best offer. While cash can offer certain advantages, experienced sellers know there's more to consider than just how the buyer is paying. 👀
The strongest offer often comes down to the complete package: closing timeline, contingencies, proof of funds, flexibility, and the buyer's ability to actually get to the closing table.
That's why smart sellers don't just look at the price or whether it's cash—they evaluate the entire offer before making a decision. 🏡
Were you surprised by this fact?

“How do I get the money?” 💰
I get this question all the time. Here’s the truth:
Cash works, but it limits you. You can only use what you have.
Private money changes the game. That’s someone in your circle, a family member, a friend, someone from church, lending you what you need. You agree on the terms together, privately. You usually need about six months: buy it, renovate it, sell it, pay them back.

“How do I get the money?” 💰
I get this question all the time. Here’s the truth:
Cash works, but it limits you. You can only use what you have.
Private money changes the game. That’s someone in your circle, a family member, a friend, someone from church, lending you what you need. You agree on the terms together, privately. You usually need about six months: buy it, renovate it, sell it, pay them back.
Most people think it’s bank loans or nothing. It’s not. I’ve done this with private money over and over, and I teach my mentees how to do the same.

feed.title: "Being pre-qualified gives you the same…
caps: linkedin

"Being pre-qualified gives you the same advantage as being pre-approved when making an offer."
Many buyers use these terms interchangeably, but understanding the difference can have a major impact on your home-buying journey. 👀
A pre-qualification is typically a quick estimate of what you may be able to afford based on information you provide. A pre-approval, on the other hand, involves a deeper review of your finances by a lender and shows sellers that you're a more serious and prepared buyer.
In a competitive market, that extra level of preparation can make a big difference when it's time to submit an offer. 💪🏡
So, what's your answer: Myth or Fact? Drop it in the comments before revealing the answer! ⬇️

feed.title: 7 rules before you make your…
caps: instagram

7 rules before you make your first real estate investment 🏡

When I was still a principal, nobody handed me a roadmap. I had to figure out what to do — and more importantly, what NOT to do — before I put a single dollar into a deal.

Here’s what I wish someone told me first 👇
1️⃣ Don’t wait until you’re “ready” — the perfect time doesn’t exist

2️⃣ Don’t invest in a market just because you live there — follow the data

3️⃣ Know your strategy BEFORE you look at properties — pick your lane first

4️⃣ Run the numbers before you fall in love — cash flow doesn’t care about the kitchen

5️⃣ Know your financing options before you make an offer — or you’ll lose the deal

6️⃣ Build your team before you need them — agent, lender, contractor on speed dial

7️⃣ Have the hard money conversation with yourself — how much can you actually invest?

Most people skip straight to browsing Zillow.

Then wonder why nothing works out. ✋🏽
The investors who win? 

They do the boring work BEFORE the deal shows up.

feed.title: 🏡 MYTH OR FACT 🏡 "The…
caps: linkedin

🏡 MYTH OR FACT 🏡
"The property with the highest cap rate is always the best investment."
This is one of those questions that separates investors who know the numbers from investors who know the business. 👀
A high cap rate might look great at first glance, but experienced investors know that's only one piece of the puzzle. The best deals aren't always the ones with the biggest returns on paper—they're the ones that align with your goals, market conditions, risk tolerance, and long-term strategy.
Before you invest, ask yourself:
✔️ Is the property in a strong market?
✔️ What are the vacancy rates?
✔️ Is there appreciation potential?
✔️ What are the maintenance and management costs?
✔️ Does it fit my overall portfolio strategy?
The most successful investors look beyond a single metric and analyze the complete picture. 📈🏡
So, what's your answer: Myth or Fact? Drop it in the comments before swiping for the answer! ⬇️
